instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `check-keys` that takes a property-list expression and a list of required keyword keys; the plist is evaluated once and an error is signaled if any required key's GETF lookup is nil. Returns the plist on success.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 OPTIONS))
            (UNLESS (GETF #:G1 :NAME) (ERROR "Missing required key: ~S" :NAME))
            (UNLESS (GETF #:G1 :TYPE) (ERROR "Missing required key: ~S" :TYPE))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 OPTIONS))
            (UNLESS (GETF #:G112 :NAME)
              (ERROR "Missing required key: ~S" :NAME))
            (UNLESS (GETF #:G112 :TYPE)
              (ERROR "Missing required key: ~S" :TYPE))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
